message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-017 in src/assay_engine/provenance.py
What: documented serialized records don't embed keying; verifier supplies the secret out-of-band.
Test(s): (doc; no test)
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	
219	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
220	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
221	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
222	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
223	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
224	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
225	    """
226	    return tuple(
227	        {
228	            "seq": e.seq,
229	            "kind": e.kind,
230	            "summary": e.summary,
231	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
232	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
233	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
234	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
235	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
236	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
237	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
238	        }
239	        for e in entries
240	    )
241	
242	
243	def verify_records(
244	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
245	    *,
246	    secret: bytes | None = None,
247	) -> None:
248	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
249	
250	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
251	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
252	    """
253	    prev = _GENESIS
254	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
255	        if e.seq != i:
256	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
257	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
258	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
259	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
260	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
261	            raise ProvenanceError(
262	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
263	            )
264	        prev = e.entry_hash
265	
266	
267	def from_records(
268	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
269	    *,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
272	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
273	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
274	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
275	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
276	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
277	        if missing:
278	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
279	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
280	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
281	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
282	        rebuilt.append(
283	            ProvenanceEntry(
284	                seq=r["seq"],
285	                kind=r["kind"],
286	                summary=r["summary"],
287	                payload=r["payload"],
288	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
289	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
290	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
291	            )
292	        )
293	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
294	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
295	    return entries
296

content.text:
This is a documentation fix (H-017). Let me verify the documented claim is accurate against the actual code behavior. The claim has several technical assertions I should check.

The docstring at lines 219-224 claims:
1. Keyed and unkeyed trails serialize identically (records carry no key material, no algorithm marker)
2. An algorithm marker would be spoofable since it's not part of the per-entry digest
3. The verifier must know out-of-band whether keyed, and supply the same secret
4. `verify_records` fails closed under wrong/absent secret

Let me verify each against the code.

input.description: Show files in the H-017 batch commit

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -p -S "Keying is NOT embedded" -- src/assay_engine/provenance.py | head -60
input.description: Find commit that introduced the H-017 docstring

content.content:
commit 011942ba0d024960245c8ab919fdf86388cff686
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 01:31:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
    
    Pass-5 confirmed findings (2-agent verified after self-refutation; revert-discriminated):
    
    Reliability / error contracts:
    - H-004 (med) malformed reasoning params (temperature/seed) raise the typed
      PermanentReasoningError instead of a raw ValueError/TypeError leaking past run()/run_json.
    - H-021 (low) QdrantVectorStore.query rejects non-positive k and skips returned points
      lacking our 'ref' payload (was an opaque KeyError).
    - H-022 (low) vectorstore int env vars parse via _int_env, naming the bad var instead of an
      opaque import-time "invalid literal for int()".
    - H-013 (low) the provenance-trail artifact tempfile is unlinked after the tracker copies it
      (was delete=False, leaked per tracked run).
    
    CI / supply-chain:
    - H-007 (med) license gate surfaces UNKNOWN/empty-license packages as a ::warning:: (hidden-
      copyleft vector) instead of passing them silently; still fails only on explicit deny matches.
    - H-008 (med) workflow pins GITHUB_TOKEN to least-privilege `permissions: contents: read`.
    - H-009 (med) the integration job verifies the Postgres/Qdrant service containers are
      reachable (pg_isready + /readyz) so the live tests can't silently SKIP and pass vacuously.
    
    Observability / governance:
    - H-011 (med) documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry between the graph gate
      node and run_study (the runner owns + always records to the trail; the node bridges).
    - H-017 (low) documented that serialized provenance records don't embed keying — the verifier
      supplies the secret out-of-band (key management; verify_records fails closed).
    
    Documentation honesty (assertion-vs-implementation):
    - H-010 (med) stable_seed: "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" corrected to the birthday
      bound of the truncated space (not a uniqueness guarantee).
    - H-012 (low) gatenode "atomically"/"terminal idempotency" claims scoped honestly.
    - H-023 (low) redact_creds "always scrubbed precisely" corrected to "precise when the conn
      string appears verbatim" (reformatted strings fall to the regex backstops).
    - H-018 (med) transcript scrubber redacts the username in the project-dir slug at any length.
    - H-024 (low) the in-process unfreeze test is marked illustrative; the seed-pinned subprocess
      test is the discriminating guard.

011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/provenance.py b/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
index 7a76678..39c6c75 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
@@ -215,6 +215,13 @@ def entries_to_records(
 
     The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
     returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
+
+    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
+    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
+    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
+    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
+    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
+    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
     """
     return tuple(
         {

content.text:
The change is purely the docstring addition. Now let me verify each factual claim against the actual implementation.

**Claim 1: records carry no key material and no algorithm marker.** Confirmed — `entries_to_records` (lines 226-240) emits only seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, entry_hash. No secret, no algorithm field.

**Claim 2: the digest input contains no algorithm marker** (so any added marker would be spoofable). Confirmed — `_digest` (lines 79-91) hashes `["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]`. No "keyed/unkeyed" or algorithm flag is in the digested message. So a marker added to the record dict would not be covered by the hash — an attacker could flip it. The claim is technically sound.

**Claim 3: keyed and unkeyed serialize identically.** The record *structure* is identical; only `entry_hash` values differ (SHA-256 vs HMAC), which is correct and consistent with the claim (the claim is about format/fields, not the hash bytes).

**Claim 4: `verify_records` fails closed under wrong/absent secret.** Confirmed — line 259-263 recomputes `_digest` with the supplied `secret` and uses `hmac.compare_digest`; if the trail was HMAC-keyed and `secret=None` (or wrong), the recomputed SHA-256/HMAC won't match and it raises `ProvenanceError`. Fails closed.

69	def test_keyed_trail_cannot_be_reforged_without_secret():
70	    secret = b"provenance-seal-secret-0123456789"
71	    t = ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=_fixed_clock())
72	    t.record("verdict", "H1: SUPPORTED", label="SUPPORTED")
73	    recs = list(t.to_records())
74	    # a forger edits the payload and recomputes the chain — but WITHOUT the secret, using the
75	    # unkeyed path — the keyed verify rejects it.
76	    forged = from_records  # alias
77	    tampered = [{**recs[0], "payload": {"label": "CONTRADICTED"}}]
78	    # recompute as an UNKEYED chain (forger has no secret):
79	    rebuilt = ProvenanceTrail(clock=_fixed_clock())
80	    rebuilt.record("verdict", "H1: CONTRADICTED", label="CONTRADICTED")
81	    forged_recs = rebuilt.to_records()
82	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
83	        forged(forged_recs, secret=secret)  # does not verify under the real key
84	    # the legitimate keyed trail verifies with the secret, and NOT without it
85	    assert from_records(recs, secret=secret)
86	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
87	        from_records(recs)  # unkeyed verify of a keyed trail fails
88	    _ = tampered
89

content.text:
The existing test corroborates the documented behavior: a keyed trail's records verify only with the secret (line 85), fail without it (lines 86-87), and a forger's unkeyed reseal is rejected under the real key (line 82-83). This is exactly the "fails closed under wrong/absent secret" and "verifier must supply the same secret out-of-band" claim.

This is a doc-only finding (no test required for the fix itself). I evaluate whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest.

Assessment:
- The docstring accurately describes the actual serialization format (no key material, no algorithm marker — verified against `entries_to_records` lines 226-240).
- The "spoofable marker" reasoning is technically correct: `_digest` (lines 79-91) does not include any algorithm/keying flag in the hashed message, so an out-of-band marker added to records would not be integrity-protected. Honest framing of a real design subtlety.
- "verify_records fails closed under the wrong/absent secret" is backed by lines 259-263 and demonstrated by `test_keyed_trail_cannot_be_reforged_without_secret`.
- It correctly frames this as deliberate key management, not a gap — consistent with the module-level "honest scope" docstring (lines 16-28) and ADR-0009 reference.
- The cross-references (`from_records`/`verify_records`) are accurate.

No regression: change is comment/docstring only; no behavior altered (confirmed by the diff). The claim is honest and matches implementation.

input.reasoning:
H-017 is a doc-only finding; the change (commit 011942b) adds only a docstring paragraph to entries_to_records in src/assay_engine/provenance.py (lines 219-224) — no behavioral code changed (verified via git show diff), so no regression is possible.

I verified every factual claim in the new docstring against the implementation:
1. "records carry no key material and no algorithm marker" — CONFIRMED: entries_to_records (lines 226-240) emits only seq/kind/summary/payload/timestamp/prev_hash/entry_hash; no secret, no algorithm field.
2. "a marker would be spoofable since it is not part of the per-entry digest" — CONFIRMED: _digest (lines 79-91) hashes ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp] with no keying/algorithm flag, so any record-level marker would be outside integrity protection.
3. "keyed and unkeyed serialize identically (same fields)" — CONFIRMED: only entry_hash bytes differ (SHA-256 vs HMAC); structure is identical.
4. "verifier must supply the same secret out-of-band; verify_records fails closed under wrong/absent secret" — CONFIRMED: verify_records (lines 259-263) recomputes _digest with the supplied secret and hmac.compare_digest's it; and the existing test test_keyed_trail_cannot_be_reforged_without_secret (tests/test_provenance.py:69-88) demonstrates a keyed trail verifies only with the secret (line 85) and raises ProvenanceError without it (lines 86-87) and against a forger's unkeyed reseal (lines 82-83).

The documented claim is accurate, honest, and consistent with the module-level honest-scope docstring (lines 16-28) and ADR-0009. As a doc item, no discriminating test is required; behavior backing the claim is already independently tested.

content.text: CONFIRMED. H-017 is a doc-only fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/provenance.py (docstring added to `entries_to_records`, lines 219-224). Every factual claim is accurate against the implementation, the change is comment-only with no regression risk, and the documented fail-closed behavior is independently backed by `test_keyed_trail_cannot_be_reforged_without_secret` in tests/test_provenance.py.
